designs.mechanic: An irregular grid has empty cells of varying shapes; drag puzzle-piece fragments into the empty spaces to fill the grid without gaps or overlaps. When the last piece locks in and the grid is complete, the entire surface glows and dims to black.

designs.name: INK FILL
designs.conditioned_path: paint-fill and ink-drip ASMR where the color always stops before the last corner
designs.mechanic: An outlined shape sits empty; hold your thumb to let ink spread from your touch point and flood-fill the outline. Lift too early and a gap remains; hold until ink reaches every edge and the outline dissolves into a clean solid shape.

designs.name: DECLUTTER TAP
designs.conditioned_path: minimalism and throw-away ASMR that lingers on the crowded surface before release
designs.mechanic: A shelf of objects appears; some have a faint grey glow marking them as clutter. Tap each glowing object to remove it with a gentle poof. When only the essential items remain and the shelf breathes, the session closes with a quiet exhale sound.

designs.name: MATCH THE GAP
designs.conditioned_path: spacing-check and whitespace-OCD in design tutorials that zoom in on uneven margins
designs.mechanic: Three objects sit in a row with one gap visibly wrong; drag the middle object left or right until the two gaps are exactly equal. When the gaps match within a pixel, the objects lock and the uneven tension releases into a satisfied chime.

designs.name: RULE REVEAL
designs.conditioned_path: niche deep-dive rabbit-holes where the algorithm rewards hyper-specific rule-hunting without ever surfacing the actual rule
designs.mechanic: A sequence of colored shapes appears; tap the next shape in the pattern before the blank slot fades. The rule is never stated — you discover it by watching the sequence repeat and vary. When you've correctly predicted 10 in a row the rule surfaces as text and the session closes.
designs.video_trick: curiosity gap — the rule is right there if you look close enough

designs.name: SEQUENCE LOCK
designs.conditioned_path: hyper-specific procedure loops — the forum thread that lays out the exact 12-step ritual for something obscure
designs.mechanic: A combination lock shows 6 dials; each dial has a hidden rule for which symbol it cycles to next. Tap each dial in the correct sequence to open the lock. No guidance — pattern-watch two full cycles, then execute. Click, click, click, open. A finite vault with a clean end.

designs.name: SORT BY RULE
designs.conditioned_path: taxonomy rabbit-holes — the Reddit thread classifying every sub-type of a thing with rigorous criteria
designs.mechanic: Items drift in and you sort them into bins by a rule you must infer from the first few items. Each correct sort reveals more of the rule; each wrong sort gently returns the item. When all 20 items are correctly sorted the rule prints and the tray seals.
designs.video_trick: shock-honesty — the rule was hiding in plain sight the whole time

designs.name: PATTERN FILL
designs.conditioned_path: satisfying-completion loops — mosaic, pixel-art fill, or number-colour-by-number that the algorithm feeds endlessly
designs.mechanic: A grid of cells has a hidden repeating tile pattern with gaps. Tap each empty cell to fill it in the color the pattern demands. Consistent audio click per correct fill; a wrong tap greys out. When the grid is complete it pulses once and the session ends.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the pattern resolves to whole

designs.name: TRAIN TIMETABLE
designs.conditioned_path: system-mastery loops — the algorithm that surfaces transit-nerd, schedule-nerd, or logistics-nerd content to people who love knowing exactly how a system ticks
designs.mechanic: A timetable grid shows departure slots; drag each train token into its correct departure slot so no two trains share a platform at the same time. The rule is the constraint system itself — figure it out, fill it in, and the last train departs on a clean chime.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — every slot filled, system running smooth

designs.name: COLOR TAXONOMY
designs.conditioned_path: colour-theory and palette rabbit-holes — the deep-dives on hue families and complementary systems
designs.mechanic: Swatches arrive one at a time; drag each into the correct position on a colour wheel taxonomy. There are no labels — only the relationship between neighbours tells you where a swatch belongs. A perfectly placed swatch hums softly; a wrong placement nudges back. 24 swatches, one clean wheel.
designs.video_trick: near-perfection itch — almost right, nudge it one slot

designs.name: PRIME SIEVE
designs.conditioned_path: number-pattern rabbit-holes — the maths-nerd thread walking through elegant integer sequences
designs.mechanic: A grid of numbers 1-50 lights up one by one; tap each composite to grey it out, leaving only primes glowing. There is no timer — just you and the sieve of Eratosthenes, a 2,000-year-old rule you discover (or remember) at your own pace. When all composites are greyed the primes pulse in unison.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the primes emerge from the noise

designs.name: ECHO SEQUENCE
designs.conditioned_path: audio-pattern loops — ASMR tap sequences and rhythmic repeat content that rewards sustained sensory attention
designs.mechanic: A sequence of tones plays; tap the pads in the same order to echo it back. Each correct echo plays the sequence one note longer. The audio is the entire feedback loop — precise, predictable, and never random. Reach the full 12-note sequence and it resolves to a chord.
designs.video_trick: participation-comment-bait — how far did you echo?

designs.name: CATEGORY CENSUS
designs.conditioned_path: classification rabbit-holes — the thread sorting every entry of a thing into a precise taxonomy with argued edge cases
designs.mechanic: Items drop from above; tap the correct category bin for each. The bins are labelled but the items are ambiguous — a few rounds reveal which edge cases belong where. Each correctly categorised item clicks into its bin; a wrong bin gently bounces it back. 30 items, then the census is complete.
designs.video_trick: shock-honesty — that edge case belonged in the other bin all along

designs.name: ROUTINE BUILDER
designs.conditioned_path: optimised-routine content — the morning-routine, workflow-setup, and system-design deep-dives the algorithm feeds to routine-mastery brains
designs.mechanic: Drag step cards into a vertical sequence to build a routine. Each card snaps with a satisfying click; misplaced cards push back softly. A dependency checker lights green when all steps are in the correct logical order. Lock the routine and a clean summary card slides down — your system, complete.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the routine locked and running

designs.name: GRID LOGIC
designs.conditioned_path: logic-puzzle rabbit-holes — Sudoku, nonogram, and Picross deep-dives the algorithm feeds to constraint-solving brains
designs.mechanic: A 5x5 grid has clues on each row and column (counts of filled cells). Tap cells to fill or unfill them until all clue constraints are satisfied. No guessing — pure deductive logic. Each satisfied row or column softly highlights. Last cell in, the grid blooms.
designs.video_trick: near-perfection itch — one cell left, the answer is right there

designs.name: SIGNAL SORT
designs.conditioned_path: signal-vs-noise rabbit-holes — the thread identifying the one real pattern buried in a noisy dataset
designs.mechanic: A stream of icons flows across the canvas; tap only the ones that match a hidden signal pattern. The noise icons are similar but subtly wrong — wrong rotation, wrong count of dots, wrong gap. Each correct tap adds a soft chime; each miss adds a subtle dissonant tone. 20 correct picks and the signal prints.
designs.video_trick: play-along comment bait — can you spot the signal by round 5?

designs.name: TYPE SORT
designs.conditioned_path: typology rabbit-holes — MBTI, Enneagram, plant genus, bird species ID threads the algorithm surfaces to systemising brains
designs.mechanic: Specimens slide in one at a time (abstract shapes with coded traits). Drag each into the correct type-bucket based on a key you build up as you go — early specimens are textbook examples, later ones are edge cases. 24 specimens. When the last one slots in, every bucket shows its complete set.

designs.name: BEAT MAP
designs.conditioned_path: music-structure rabbit-holes — the deep-dives into song construction, verse-chorus maps, and time-signature analysis
designs.mechanic: A song structure map loads as empty bars. Listen to a short audio loop and tap each bar to label it (intro/verse/chorus/bridge/outro) based on what you hear. Correct labels snap with a satisfying tick; mismatches push back. Map the full structure and the song plays through once more, labelled.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the map that explains the song

designs.name: PIPELINE TRACE
designs.conditioned_path: systems-diagram rabbit-holes — the flowchart and process-map content the algorithm feeds to pipeline-obsessed brains
designs.mechanic: A tangle of pipe segments sits on the canvas; tap each segment to rotate it until water can flow from source to drain without leaks. Each connected segment glows; each completed path fills with a gentle flow animation. All paths flowing is the end state.

designs.name: ODD ONE DEEP
designs.conditioned_path: pattern-deviation rabbit-holes — the thread cataloguing every exception to a rule in a system
designs.mechanic: A matrix of items has one that violates the system rule. The rule is not stated. Watch the matrix for one full cycle, then tap the outlier. Each round adds a dimension (shape AND colour AND count AND rotation) — the rule grows more specific, the outlier more subtle. 10 rounds and the outlier chain resolves.
designs.video_trick: play-along comment bait — did you catch the outlier before round 7?

designs.name: FREQUENCY STACK
designs.conditioned_path: data-visualisation rabbit-holes — the thread mapping frequency distributions and histograms of niche datasets
designs.mechanic: Tokens fall and you drag each to the correct frequency column on a histogram you build in real time. The distribution has a hidden shape (bell curve, skew, bimodal) you discover only as you place items. When the last token is placed the histogram shows its shape and a label prints — you figured out the distribution.
designs.video_trick: shock-honesty — the shape was bimodal the whole time

designs.name: SENSE CALIBRATE
designs.conditioned_path: sensory-processing content — threads on sound sensitivity, texture grading, and sensory preference mapping
designs.mechanic: A soft tone plays; slide a dial to the exact frequency that matches it. The dial gives consistent haptic-style visual feedback as you approach the target — a waveform that settles to flat when aligned. 8 tones, each a different frequency. Last tone calibrated, the dial sequence plays back as a chord.
designs.video_trick: near-perfection itch — almost flat, one hair to the left

designs.name: MAP LEGEND
designs.conditioned_path: cartography and classification rabbit-holes — the deep-dive on map symbology, legend systems, and geographic taxonomy
designs.mechanic: A map displays regions marked with unknown symbols; a legend panel shows symbol definitions in scrambled order. Drag each legend label to its correct symbol. The map's symbols are consistent — once you decode one region, the pattern cascades. All labels matched, the map unlocks its full annotated view.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the map finally makes sense

designs.name: SYSTEM CLOSE
designs.conditioned_path: system-mastery niche loops — the deep-dive that pulls you in because you need to see the complete system before you can stop
designs.mechanic: A 5-stage machine shows its gears; each stage has a hidden trigger condition. Discover and satisfy each condition in order — the machine advances one stage at a time with a satisfying mechanical click. All 5 stages triggered, the machine runs a full cycle and prints SYSTEM COMPLETE. Share your run count.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the machine runs its first clean cycle

designs.name: BREATH DIAL
designs.conditioned_path: doomscrolling as a nervous-system coping loop — chasing the next card to feel in control
designs.mechanic: A single dial sits center-screen; drag it clockwise to inhale (circle expands, tone rises), counterclockwise to exhale (circle contracts, tone falls). The dial moves only as fast as you drag — you set every pace. No wrong speed, no failure state. Five full slow cycles and the session softly closes.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the circle expanding and contracting is the whole reveal

designs.name: ANCHOR HOLD
designs.conditioned_path: the compulsive thumb-check — opening the app to feel grounded, landing in more chaos
designs.mechanic: A glowing anchor sits on a dark sea. Hold your thumb on it: while held, the water calms, ripples still, and a progress arc fills slowly. Release early and the water stirs again; hold to the end of the arc and the screen settles to flat black with a soft chime. One hold, one resolution.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the stilling sea is the payoff

designs.name: WORRY SORT
designs.conditioned_path: the news-feed scan for reassurance — reading headlines to confirm the world is still manageable
designs.mechanic: Soft text cards drift up labeled with abstract worry words. Tap each to gently place it into one of two jars: "in my control" or "not in my control." No wrong answers, no timer. When all cards are sorted, both jars seal with a quiet click and the screen rests. Control imposed on chaos.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — both jars filling is the satisfying close

designs.name: RIPPLE TAP
designs.conditioned_path: the refresh lever — pulling to trigger a predictable response because unpredictability is unbearable
designs.mechanic: A still water surface fills the screen. Tap anywhere and a ripple expands outward perfectly, predictably, always the same. Every tap produces the exact same beautiful ring. No variation, no surprise, no score. The nervous system learns: tap, ripple, safe. Tap as many times as you want; there is no end state.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the perfect predictable ripple is the hook

designs.name: ORDER RESTORE
designs.conditioned_path: the compulsive re-read of a thread to catch everything and feel nothing was missed
designs.mechanic: A small grid of tiles is shuffled into disorder. Drag any tile to any open slot — all moves are reversible, nothing locks until you want it to. A gentle shimmer shows when the grid is fully ordered; tap DONE to close it. No timer, no wrong moves, no fail state. Restore it at your own pace.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the shimmer signaling order restored

designs.name: RHYTHM TRACE
designs.conditioned_path: the looping ASMR scroll where you replay the same soothing clip hoping the calm sticks this time
designs.mechanic: A dotted line pulses across the screen in a slow, perfectly predictable wave. Trace it with your thumb — the line glows wherever you match it. There is no score, no gap penalty, no miss. The line repeats the same pattern indefinitely until you lift your thumb. Gentle control of a predictable rhythm.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the glowing trail you leave behind

designs.name: SAFE LIST
designs.conditioned_path: pinning saved posts as a security blanket — hoarding reassuring content to return to
designs.mechanic: A blank list sits on screen with a soft prompt: "what do you know for certain right now?" Tap to add one soft tile at a time — each tile is a gentle color block, no words required. The list fills from the bottom. When you stop adding, tap CLOSE and the list folds away like a notebook. You made the list; you ended it.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — closing the notebook is the ending ritual

designs.name: SLOW POUR
designs.conditioned_path: the pull-to-refresh lever used as a self-soothing gesture when nothing new helps but the gesture comforts
designs.mechanic: Hold to tip a vessel; liquid pours at exactly the speed you tilt. Too fast and it splashes; too slow and it barely drips. The session is about finding the middle — a steady, predictable flow into the vessel below. When the vessel is full it chimes once and rests. Full control of the pour rate.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the vessel reaching full is the exhale

designs.name: CLOUD NAME
designs.conditioned_path: doomscrolling for reassurance — searching for named threats to contain them
designs.mechanic: Soft clouds drift across the screen. Tap each cloud to name it with a one-tap label ("work", "health", "future"). Once named, the cloud turns a calm pastel and floats off the top of the screen. When the sky is empty, new clouds drift in slowly. You control which you name and when; none vanish until you name them.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — a clear sky after the last cloud floats away

designs.name: PULSE MATCH
designs.conditioned_path: the news-refresh loop — checking for updates to synchronize inner state with outer reality
designs.mechanic: A slow pulse glows at the center of the screen. Tap in time with it — not to beat it, just to match it. If you match for three pulses in a row the pulse slows one notch. Keep matching and keep slowing it until the pulse is barely visible and the screen dims to black. You decelerate the beat by following it.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the beat you slowed to stillness

designs.name: FENCE WALK
designs.conditioned_path: the predictable-feed craving — seeking a feed that only shows familiar things, no surprises
designs.mechanic: A path stretches forward on a gentle loop; drag your thumb to walk it. The path always curves the same way, reveals the same soft scenery — a fence, a tree, a gate. Nothing unexpected ever appears. The walk has no destination and no end unless you lift your thumb. Predictable sensory input, fully controllable pace.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the reassuring sameness of the loop

designs.name: UNDO STACK
designs.conditioned_path: the re-read-before-send anxiety loop — drafting, deleting, redrafting because nothing feels safe to commit
designs.mechanic: Soft action tiles stack up as you tap them. Every action is visible in a stack on the right. Swipe the stack to undo any step — every move is fully reversible in any order. There is no wrong sequence, no commit button unless you choose. The whole point is that nothing is permanent until you want it to be.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the satisfaction of knowing every step can be taken back

designs.name: GROUND FIVE
designs.conditioned_path: the sensory-scan doomscroll — scrolling to find something that makes the body feel present and safe
designs.mechanic: Five sense prompts appear one at a time — SEE, HEAR, FEEL, SMELL, TASTE — each on a soft card. Tap the card to confirm you've noticed that sense right now. Each confirmed tap fills a gentle arc. When all five arcs are full the screen glows warmly for three seconds and closes. The 5-4-3-2-1 grounding technique as a one-thumb session.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the final warm glow when all five senses are named

designs.name: TIDE PULL
designs.conditioned_path: the pull-to-refresh conditioned gesture — the swipe down that promises something new but delivers more chaos
designs.mechanic: Pull down slowly and the tide rises up the screen; release and it ebbs back. The tide always behaves the same way — no variables, no surprise. Pull to bring it in, release to let it out. After six slow pull-release cycles the tide settles at a calm midline and the session ends with a soft wash sound. The gesture redeemed.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — six tides and then the water settles

designs.name: SOFT CHECK
designs.conditioned_path: the compulsive notification check — opening the app to confirm everything is okay and closing having seen nothing that helps
designs.mechanic: A single soft circle sits on screen labeled CHECK. Tap it once: a gentle "all clear" animation plays — a slow checkmark draws itself, a soft chime sounds, the circle pulses green. That is the whole session. One tap, one confirmation, done. The check is granted immediately, reliably, every time. No feed, no scroll, just the check.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the checkmark that actually resolves the loop

designs.name: STORM CLEAR
designs.conditioned_path: news-feed scanning for threats — the loop of checking to see if anything new is bad
designs.mechanic: A storm of icon-sized threat words rains down the screen. Tap each to neutralize it — it dissolves into a soft ring. Clear them all before they pile up below the line. When the sky is empty a calm horizon fades in and your "cleared" count is your dare score. The anxiety discharge made finite and winnable.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the clear sky unlocking after the last word dissolves

designs.name: CONTROL TOWER
designs.conditioned_path: the god-complex feed-curation loop — long-pressing to teach the algorithm, seeking the fantasy of total feed control
designs.mechanic: Inbound events stream across a radar screen. Tap each to reroute it safely before it reaches the center. Events are color-coded by type — predictable, fully legible, no surprises. Score is events routed without a center breach. The player is the air traffic controller — nothing happens without their approval.
designs.video_trick: benchmark ego bait — "I cleared 47 without a breach"

designs.name: SAFE COUNT
designs.conditioned_path: the reassurance-seeking scroll — counting wins, good news, safe signals in a feed that keeps the alarm on
designs.mechanic: Safe-signal tiles (soft green shapes) and threat tiles (soft red) drift down mixed together. Tap only the green ones to bank them before they reach the bottom. Miss a green and lose one from your tally; tap a red and your hand shakes for a second. Score is the highest safe-count you reach. Finding the safe signals and naming them — bounded, winnable.
designs.video_trick: near-perfection itch — the almost-missed green tile that haunts the retry

designs.name: KNOWN NEXT
designs.conditioned_path: the story-bar scan — checking who posted so you know exactly what comes next before you watch
designs.mechanic: A sequence of colored tiles is revealed one at a time in a known pattern. After four tiles the player must predict tile five by tapping a color. Correct: sequence continues and builds. Wrong: the predictable pattern is broken and the session ends. Score is the longest streak of correct predictions — comforted by knowing what comes next.
designs.video_trick: participation-comment-bait — "call the next color before it flips"

designs.name: PLAN THE DAY
designs.conditioned_path: the productive-scroll loop — consuming planning content to feel prepared without actually committing to anything
designs.mechanic: A blank day-grid of six soft time-blocks sits on screen. Drag a pre-made activity tile (rest, move, eat, connect, work, play) into any block. No wrong arrangement, no judgment. Swap tiles freely — all moves reversible. When you tap DONE the grid softly locks, the session closes, and the day has a shape. Control imposed on uncertainty.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the locked grid, the day with a visible shape

designs.name: SHELF FILL
designs.conditioned_path: haul unboxing videos that show a shelf slowly populating with a set
designs.mechanic: Tap floating items one at a time to place them onto a shelf grid. Each item lands with a soft click and the shelf fills left-to-right, top-to-bottom. When the last slot is filled the shelf glows gently and the session ends — a finite set, completely yours.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the shelf full at last

designs.name: SET SNAP
designs.conditioned_path: gacha unboxing where each pull dangles the missing piece of the set
designs.mechanic: Tap to draw a card from a no-cash deck of 12. Each new card snaps into a visible set grid; duplicates highlight in red so you feel the missing slots. Pull until the full set is complete — the deck has a bottom and every slot fills.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the last card snapping into place

designs.name: GRID COMPLETE
designs.conditioned_path: bingo / collection-tracker grids that keep one square empty forever
designs.mechanic: A 5x5 grid of soft-colored tiles sits on screen, most dim. Tap a tile to light it; tiles light in a hidden unlock order revealed one hint at a time. Fill all 25 and the grid ripples once and holds — done, no new tiles, no infinite expansion.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — every cell finally lit

designs.name: TYPE DEX
designs.conditioned_path: Pokédex / trading-card completionist feeds that dangle the one card you don’t have
designs.mechanic: Silhouettes of 20 creatures drift across the screen; tap each type icon that matches the silhouette to capture it. Capture all 20 and the dex seals with a satisfying full-roster flash. Each captured entry is free, and the roster has exactly 20.

designs.name: GARDEN GROW
designs.conditioned_path: plant-collection and seed-haul videos that always tease the next species
designs.mechanic: Tap soil patches to plant seeds; each tap costs one water drop from a finite daily jar. Seeds sprout into one of 10 plant species displayed on a completion bar. When all 10 species are grown the garden is full and the session closes peacefully.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the garden finished, nothing more to add

designs.name: STICKER BOOK
designs.conditioned_path: sticker-collection hauls and blind-pack openings that sell the incomplete album
designs.mechanic: Sticker tiles flutter in one at a time; tap each to peel and place it on the correct blank in the album spread. No duplicates, no blank that never fills. When the last sticker lands the album shuts with a soft snap and the session ends.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the full album, finally sealed

designs.name: COLOR SET
designs.conditioned_path: palette-collection content where creators unbox every shade of a makeup line
designs.mechanic: Swatches drift across the canvas; tap each one to add it to a growing palette row. The palette has exactly 16 slots — one per hue in the spectrum. Fill the row left to right and the completed rainbow palette glows softly and holds still.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the full spectrum, side by side

designs.name: STAMP RALLY
designs.conditioned_path: loyalty-stamp cards and punch-card FOMO that dangle the free reward one stamp away
designs.mechanic: Stamp icons appear one at a time on a 3x3 card; tap each before it fades to claim it. Stamp all 9 to redeem the card and see the full set revealed. The card has exactly 9 slots and the deck is finite at 3 cards total — 27 taps and it’s done.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the final stamp, the card complete

designs.name: MINERAL HUNT
designs.conditioned_path: rock and crystal unboxing hauls that tease the missing specimen in a collection
designs.mechanic: Tap to chip a tile off a stone face; each chip reveals a fragment of a mineral specimen. Chip enough to identify it and it adds to a display case of 8 specimens. Find all 8 types and the case locks with a complete collection badge. No infinite pull, just 8 hidden minerals.
designs.video_trick: reveal bait — what mineral is hiding under this rock?

designs.name: HAUL UNPACK
designs.conditioned_path: unboxing haul videos where each item is revealed with mounting anticipation
designs.mechanic: A box sits on screen; swipe down to open the flap and an item reveals with a pop animation. Each item goes straight to a visible collection shelf. The box contains exactly 10 items, no random-rare gatekeeping. Open all 10 and the haul is complete.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the shelf full after the last item

designs.name: PATCH BOARD
designs.conditioned_path: merit badge and achievement patch boards where the empty slots are always visible
designs.mechanic: A board shows 12 patch outlines. Small missions drift across the screen as simple one-tap prompts; completing each earns a patch that fills the corresponding outline. No timers, no grinding. Fill all 12 and the board is yours, complete.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the board full, no empty outlines

designs.name: SPECIES LOG
designs.conditioned_path: bird-watching and wildlife photography posts that tease a life-list never finished
designs.mechanic: Silhouette cards drift past like sightings; tap each silhouette you recognize to log it in a field guide of 15 species. Each logged species fills a page in a finite guide. When all 15 pages are filled the guide closes and the session ends.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the life-list closed, nothing missing

designs.name: NO-CASH GACHA
designs.conditioned_path: gacha and loot-box content that dangles pulls behind paywalls and rates
designs.mechanic: Tap the capsule to spin a free pull; every pull is guaranteed unique from a set of 15 collectibles shown in a tracker. Duplicate protection means each tap makes progress. The 15th pull fills the tracker and the machine locks — complete, no premium currency, no infinite loop.
designs.video_trick: variable reward with a guaranteed end — the pull that completes it

designs.name: COMPLETION METER
designs.conditioned_path: progress bars on social profiles that sit at 85% and itch for one more action
designs.mechanic: A progress bar shows a collection at 80% complete. Scattered collectibles fall across the screen; tap each to add it to the bar. The bar climbs to 100%, the collectibles stop falling, and the meter seals with a final ding. 100% is reachable in under 2 minutes.
designs.video_trick: near-perfection itch — the meter at 95% screams for one more tap

designs.name: VAULT SORT
designs.conditioned_path: collection organization and sorting videos where chaos becomes order
designs.mechanic: A pile of 18 collectible tiles tumbles onto the screen; drag each into the matching labeled vault slot. No timer, no score — just the satisfaction of every item in its place. When the last tile slots in the vault door closes softly and the session ends.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the vault organized and sealed

designs.name: RARE CATCH
designs.conditioned_path: rare-find hunting content where creators tease the one item that completes a set
designs.mechanic: Items fall fast across the screen; tap only the ones matching the “missing” highlight shown at the top to add them to your set tracker. Common items are decoys. Catch all 6 rare targets and the tracker fills to 100%. A fixed session of 90 seconds and exactly 6 rares hidden in the stream.
designs.video_trick: rare reveal — the moment the missing piece appears

designs.name: RECIPE SET
designs.conditioned_path: recipe collection and cookbook hauls where one dish is always missing from the repertoire
designs.mechanic: Ingredient tiles drift across the screen; tap each one to add it to a recipe card. Each full recipe card unlocks and files into a recipe book with 8 slots. Fill the book’s 8 recipe slots and the book snaps shut — a finite, free, completable cookbook.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the recipe book full and filed

designs.name: BADGE COLLECT
designs.conditioned_path: achievement badge walls on apps that always show the one badge you haven’t earned
designs.mechanic: 10 badge outlines glow on a board. One badge lights up live; tap it to claim it before it dims. Claim it and it fills permanently. Badges light in sequence but with random timing. Claim all 10 and the board shines complete — no new badge ever appears.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the last badge claimed, the board full

designs.name: MAP PIN
designs.conditioned_path: travel map collection content where every new pin signals a place you haven’t been
designs.mechanic: A simple world-map outline sits on screen with 12 destination circles unmarked. Small destination cards drift in; tap each card then tap its matching circle on the map to pin it. Pin all 12 and the map holds the complete set — every pin placed, the journey closed.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — every pin placed, the map full

designs.name: DECK BUILD
designs.conditioned_path: trading-card deck-building content that dangles the one card completing your strategy
designs.mechanic: Cards slide in one at a time from a shuffled draw pile of 20. Tap each to add it to your hand or discard; fill a 10-card deck with one of each type shown in a tracker. The draw pile has exactly 20 cards — no infinite pack, no premium pulls. Complete the deck and it snaps face-up in a full-set spread.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the full deck laid out

designs.name: BUBBLE GRID
designs.conditioned_path: pop-it and bubble-wrap ASMR loops that deliver a pop hit on every tap
designs.mechanic: A grid of soft bubble circles fills the canvas; tap each to pop it with a satisfying synthesized micro-burst. Bubbles refill slowly in waves after the grid is cleared, giving a definite full-pop moment then a gentle reset.

designs.name: SLIME STRETCH
designs.conditioned_path: slime-stretching ASMR reels where the pull-and-snap rhythm is the entire hook
designs.mechanic: Drag a blob of generative slime upward; it stretches with elastic resistance and synthesized squeak. Release to snap it back with a satisfying thwack. The texture shifts color as it cools — a self-contained stretch-and-settle loop that ends after ten pulls.

designs.name: SAND POUR
designs.conditioned_path: kinetic-sand ASMR videos where the slow pour and piling crunch is the whole loop
designs.mechanic: Tap and hold anywhere to pour a stream of particle sand that piles with soft crunch audio. Release to stop. The pile settles physics-first with a mound shape; the canvas holds one beautiful pile before gently dispersing and ending the session.

designs.name: RIPPLE POND
designs.conditioned_path: water-ripple ASMR clips where each tap-drop spreads concentric rings and a resonant plop
designs.mechanic: Tap anywhere on a still water surface to drop a ripple with a deep synthesized plop. Rings expand and overlap, playing harmonic tones when two collide. After twelve taps the pond slowly stills and dims, signaling the end of the session.

designs.name: PEEL SHEET
designs.conditioned_path: protective-film peeling and screen-protector removal ASMR that delivers a slow tear-crackle payoff
designs.mechanic: Drag a corner of a simulated protective film slowly across the canvas; it peels with procedural crinkle audio and a satisfying ripping visual. Peel the entire sheet in one unbroken motion for a complete-session reward tone.

designs.name: CRUNCH STAMP
designs.conditioned_path: packing-foam and crunch-squish ASMR where the thumb pressing down is the whole satisfaction
designs.mechanic: Press and slowly release each packing-foam cell on a grid; each cell crumples inward with a crunch sound scaled to how hard you press. Softer press = softer crunch. Fill all twenty cells to complete the sheet and end the session.

designs.name: GOO SWIPE
designs.conditioned_path: slime-wiping and putty-spread ASMR where dragging a slow streak of goo across a surface is the hook
designs.mechanic: Drag your thumb to smear a thick stripe of generative goo across the canvas, leaving a glossy trail with a wet-drag audio cue. Swipe stripes until the entire canvas is covered in one solid color — then the screen exhales and the session ends.

designs.name: GRAIN RAKE
designs.conditioned_path: zen-garden and sand-rake ASMR where drawing clean lines through granules gives a tactile reset
designs.mechanic: Drag slowly through a field of procedural sand particles; a rake leaves parallel furrows with a soft bristle-on-grain audio. Crossing old furrows erases them. Fill the canvas with clean parallel lines to complete the garden and end the session.

designs.name: SOAP SQUEEZE
designs.conditioned_path: soap-cutting and glycerin-squish ASMR where pressing a waxy bar and hearing it crumble is the entire loop
designs.mechanic: Tap to press a procedural soap bar that deforms under your thumb with a chalky squish sound. Each press leaves a thumb-shaped dent; ten presses fully crumble the bar into a pile of shavings. The crumble animation is the reward that ends the session.

designs.name: FROST SCRATCH
designs.conditioned_path: frost-window and fogged-glass scratching ASMR where revealing what is underneath gives the squeak-and-reveal payoff
designs.mechanic: Drag across a frost-covered canvas to scratch away frost particles with a high-pitched scrape audio. The clear path widens as you drag faster or slower, pitch-shifting with speed. Uncover the full canvas to reveal a soft glow beneath and end the session.

designs.name: POP STREAK
designs.conditioned_path: pop-it challenge videos where the speed of clearing an entire board is measured and dared
designs.mechanic: A pop-it board appears. Tap every bubble as fast as possible to clear the grid. Your time is stamped and shareable as a dare. Each round adds one extra row, raising the ceiling but never the floor.

designs.name: TEXTURE TAP
designs.conditioned_path: random-texture ASMR scrolls where each new surface delivers a fresh tactile-audio hit before the feed moves on
designs.mechanic: A new procedurally-generated surface fills the screen every fifteen seconds: corduroy, velvet, bark, mesh. Tap anywhere to hear and feel the texture respond with unique synthesized audio. Five textures complete the session.

designs.name: WAX MELT
designs.conditioned_path: wax-melting and candle-drip ASMR where the slow inevitability of the drip is hypnotic
designs.mechanic: Tap the top of a procedural wax pillar to ignite it; heat radiates downward in a slow melt with drip audio cues. Swipe to rotate the pillar and guide the drip. The candle fully melts over ninety seconds, then the screen fades to a soft glow.

designs.name: TAP RAIN
designs.conditioned_path: rain-on-surface ASMR where each drop landing triggers an individual soft tap sound
designs.mechanic: Rain drops fall randomly onto the canvas, each emitting a synthesized tap on impact. Slide your thumb along the bottom to tilt the canvas left or right, directing the flow into a pool. When the pool fills, the rain slows to a stop and the session ends.

designs.name: SQUISH ROW
designs.conditioned_path: squish-toy speed-run videos where cramming every toy in a row as fast as possible is the dare
designs.mechanic: A row of squishy toys appears. Press each in order from left to right before it reinflates. Speed up across five rounds; the reinflation timer shrinks. Score is your fastest clean sweep.

designs.name: INK DROP
designs.conditioned_path: ink-in-water ASMR where a slow bloom of color spreading through liquid is the entire satisfaction
designs.mechanic: Tap anywhere on a water surface to drop a bead of ink; it blooms outward in procedural fluid simulation with a soft hiss. Each tap adds a new color that bleeds into the last. Ten drops create a full-canvas tie-dye — then the animation slowly stills.

designs.name: VELCRO RIP
designs.conditioned_path: velcro-ripping ASMR speed clips where the tear length determines the sound hit
designs.mechanic: Drag across a velcro strip as fast as possible; the rip sound scales in pitch and crackle intensity with your drag speed. Score is peak drag speed in pixels-per-second. Three rips per session, best score is the shareable dare.

designs.name: WRAP & REST
designs.conditioned_path: bubble-wrap ASMR loops that play endlessly — the game version ends on its own terms instead
designs.mechanic: A single large sheet of bubble wrap fills the canvas. Pop at your own pace — no timer, no score. After the last bubble pops the sheet folds itself away with a soft rustle and a gentle done-tone plays. The session has an actual end.

designs.name: SOUND SHAPE
designs.conditioned_path: sound-reactive ASMR and Cymatics videos where sound visibly reshapes sand or water into patterns
designs.mechanic: Tap a tone button to emit a synthesized frequency; the particle field on screen rearranges into the standing-wave pattern for that tone. Choose from five tones. Hold the last tone until the pattern locks and hums into stillness.

designs.name: CRINKLE PRESS
designs.conditioned_path: crinkle-paper and foil-pressing ASMR where the slow compress-and-release of a surface is the sensory payload
designs.mechanic: Press and slowly release anywhere on a procedural foil surface; it dents with a high-frequency crinkle audio scaled to press pressure. Lighter presses give delicate whisper crinkles; hard presses give a satisfying full crumple. Fifteen presses smooth the foil back flat and end the session.

designs.name: BEAT LOCK
designs.conditioned_path: looping audio meme that hijacks the brain into replaying the same 15-second clip on repeat
designs.mechanic: A glowing bar pulses left-to-right on a synthesized beat; tap once per pulse exactly as it peaks. Clean locks stack a visible combo multiplier. Miss one and the combo resets with a jarring off-beat flash. 32 beats, then it ends on the drop.

designs.name: DROP CATCH
designs.conditioned_path: bass-drop audio memes that condition anticipation of the exact moment the beat hits
designs.mechanic: The screen counts down with rising visual noise; tap the exact frame the drop lands. Hold too long before or after and it registers as a miss. Score is deviation in milliseconds from the drop — share your ms and dare a friend.

designs.name: TEMPO CLIMB
designs.conditioned_path: trending tempo-ramp audio where the beat accelerates until the listener physically can't keep up
designs.mechanic: Dots scroll down a lane at the beat tempo; tap each as it crosses the line. Every 8 beats the BPM notches up. Miss two in a row and the track cuts. How far up the tempo ladder can you ride before it breaks you?

designs.name: FLOW CHAIN
designs.conditioned_path: looping ASMR beats that lock the brain into a repetition trance with no exit
designs.mechanic: Tap a sequence of color-coded zones on the beat to build a chain. Each perfect hit extends the chain and deepens the visual glow. One off-beat tap breaks the chain and scores zero for that segment. 64-beat track, then it resolves.

designs.name: OFF-BEAT TRAP
designs.conditioned_path: syncopated audio memes that trip up listeners with deliberate off-beat hooks
designs.mechanic: Most beats signal with a flash; the scoring beat is silent — tap in the gap between flashes. Tapping on any lit flash scores zero. The mechanic trains the body to feel the off-beat the way the track buries it.

designs.name: BPM DUEL
designs.conditioned_path: viral 'who has better rhythm' challenge clips where two people race the same beat
designs.mechanic: A shared beat plays; your accuracy % after 32 taps is your dare score. Send the link with your score visible — the receiver sees your bar when they start, daring them to beat it. One song, one minute, settled.

designs.name: GHOST BEAT
designs.conditioned_path: layered loop tracks that add invisible percussion hits the ear barely registers
designs.mechanic: A beat plays with one ghost hit hidden in each bar — no visual cue, only ear. Tap when you feel the ghost percussion. Score is how many ghost hits you catch across 16 bars. Trains the ear the loop has been hijacking.

designs.name: PULSE HOLD
designs.conditioned_path: sustained-note audio memes where the payoff is holding the tone exactly as long as it lasts
designs.mechanic: A note sustains — press and hold for exactly its duration, then release. The error (too short or too long) is shown in ms. Ten notes of varying length per session; PB is the lowest total error.

designs.name: RHYTHM MIRROR
designs.conditioned_path: TikTok duet rhythmic challenges where one side plays a pattern and the other must mirror it
designs.mechanic: A 4-beat pattern plays on the left half of the screen; tap the right half to repeat it back perfectly. Pattern grows by one beat each round. Dare score is the longest pattern mirrored before a miss.

designs.name: DRUM FILL
designs.conditioned_path: drum-fill reaction clips where the fill is the only moment that matters in an otherwise repetitive track
designs.mechanic: A steady beat loops, then a fill window opens for 2 seconds. Tap a freeform burst of hits in that window; a groove-accuracy meter scores how well your hits land in sub-beat pockets. One fill, one score, one dare.

designs.name: SILENT BAR
designs.conditioned_path: viral 4'33" / silent-drop memes that train anticipation of a beat that never comes
designs.mechanic: A track plays normally for 3 bars then cuts to silence. Tap on the exact phantom beat where bar 4 should land. The grid shows where the beat was — your timing distance is your score. Silence as the highest-difficulty rhythm challenge.

designs.name: COMBO SUSTAIN
designs.conditioned_path: rhythm-game combo-counter clips shared as status signals of flow mastery
designs.mechanic: Tap on every beat of a 60-second track to sustain a combo counter. Each perfect tap adds to the multiplier; an early or late tap resets to zero. The session ends exactly at beat 60 — the wean is the track having a last beat.

designs.name: TAP TWIN
designs.conditioned_path: split-screen comparison clips that spark a tribal 'which side is you?' engagement loop
designs.mechanic: Two rhythm tracks play simultaneously at different tempos; tap along with whichever you choose. Your locked-in tempo vs. the one you didn't pick is the rivalry score. Share the result with your twin and see whose rhythm held.

designs.name: RHYTHM WEAN
designs.conditioned_path: seamless audio loops that never resolve — the same 8-bar phrase repeating with no end signal
designs.mechanic: A gentle looping beat plays; tap on each pulse to build a soft glowing ring. After 32 beats the ring is complete and the track resolves with a final chord — a hard, satisfying end instead of another loop. Calm flow with a real finish.

designs.name: BREATH BEAT
designs.conditioned_path: binaural / breathing-paced audio loops that hijack the inhale-exhale cycle into endless replay
designs.mechanic: A soft pulse sets the inhale; hold during the beat, release on the rest. Each matched breath-beat pair calms the visual — the screen warms and slows. After 10 matched breath cycles the track ends and the screen holds still.

designs.name: SLOW TAP
designs.conditioned_path: fast-loop audio memes that spike heart rate by compressing beat intervals
designs.mechanic: The beat starts fast and you must tap along — but each perfectly-timed tap lengthens the next interval by a fraction. You are literally slowing the track with your own precision. The session ends when the tempo reaches 40 BPM and holds.

designs.name: ONE BAR LOOP
designs.conditioned_path: one-bar loop audio memes that auto-replay exactly one measure until the listener tunes out
designs.mechanic: A single 4-beat bar repeats; tap on every beat of every loop. The challenge: how many perfect loops in a row without a miss? Each perfect loop tightens the accuracy window. 16 loops and the track signs off — the loop that ends.

designs.name: GROOVE SCORE
designs.conditioned_path: trending "rate your rhythm" challenge clips that bait self-assessment and sharing
designs.mechanic: A 30-second groove plays; tap freely to any sub-beat you feel. An AI-feel meter scores how consistently your taps align to the micro-groove vs. the macro beat. Share your Groove Score — the shareable number drives the dare.

designs.name: BEAT SKIP
designs.conditioned_path: edited loop clips that drop one beat every few bars to create the "wait, something\'s off" hook
designs.mechanic: A steady beat plays — but every 8th beat is silent. Tap on all normal beats; do NOT tap on the silent one. Your streak is how many bars you navigate without tapping the hole. The loop ends at bar 16.

designs.name: FINAL BAR
designs.conditioned_path: endless loop tracks engineered to never signal that the song is over
designs.mechanic: A short musical phrase plays and you tap along gently; after exactly 8 bars a resolution chord lands and the screen fades to soft black. Nothing loops. The whole game is the satisfaction of an 8-bar phrase that actually ends — flow state, then done.

designs.name: SEQUENCE ECHO
designs.conditioned_path: 'only 90s kids remember' nostalgia-chain posts that bait recall of childhood sequences
designs.mechanic: A colored tile sequence flashes one by one; the thumb must replay the sequence in exact order by tapping the same tiles. Each correct echo adds one more tile to the next round, growing the span until a miss ends the run and prints the final sequence length.
designs.video_trick: near-perfection itch — the last tile missed haunts the rewatch

designs.name: FLIP PAIRS
designs.conditioned_path: nostalgia-bait photo pairs ('here vs now') that farm the match-recognition reflex
designs.mechanic: A grid of face-down cards; flip two at a time to find matching pairs. All cards are cleared in one session — the score is total flip count, lower is better, daring friends to beat the memory efficiency.
designs.video_trick: competitive rank — 'I cleared it in 18 flips, beat that'

designs.name: SPOT THE CHANGE
designs.conditioned_path: 'before and after' transformation clips that train the eye to hunt for what changed
designs.mechanic: Two nearly identical screens flash alternating; tap the element that changed before the cycle repeats. Each round the change shrinks and the cycle speeds — the recall reflex is exercised, not just baited.
designs.video_trick: play-along comment bait — 'pause and spot it before I reveal'

designs.name: WORD SPAN
designs.conditioned_path: 'how many can you remember?' recall-challenge posts that spread via score-sharing
designs.mechanic: A list of words flashes for 3 seconds, then vanishes; tap the words that appeared from a larger set of distractors. The list grows by one word each round until a miss prints the working-memory span — the honest score the brain earned.
designs.video_trick: benchmark ego bait — 'I hit 9, what\'s yours?'

designs.name: GRID GHOST
designs.conditioned_path: hidden-object / 'what\'s different' clip format that parks the pattern in short-term memory
designs.mechanic: A grid of symbols lights up briefly, then goes dark; tap the cells that were lit in any order. Every correct tap scores; every wrong tap ends the round. The lit pattern grows each level, exercising spatial working memory.

designs.name: NOSTALGIA NAME
designs.conditioned_path: 'name it if you remember it' challenge posts that weaponize the recall reflex for shares
designs.mechanic: A blurred or pixelated icon from a shared-era cultural object (game, toy, show) fades in slowly; tap the correct name from three choices before it fully reveals. Score is how early you tapped — the earlier the blur-stage, the more points.
designs.video_trick: ego bait — prove you remember faster than friends

designs.name: ORDER REPLAY
designs.conditioned_path: viral 'put these in order' ranking challenges that bait debate and recall
designs.mechanic: Five items appear in random order for 4 seconds, then scramble; drag them back into the exact original order from memory. Miss-order distance is the score — lower is better, and rounds get longer lists.
designs.video_trick: participation-comment-bait — 'I got it perfect, did you?'

designs.name: COLOR CHAIN
designs.conditioned_path: trending color-sequence challenges that spread because scores are socially comparable
designs.mechanic: A chain of colored dots pulses in sequence; replay the chain by tapping the dots in the same order. Each correct replay adds one dot to the chain. The longest unbroken chain is the dare unit sent to friends.

designs.name: FACE RECALL
designs.conditioned_path: 'do you remember them?' celebrity throwback posts that farm recognition memory
designs.mechanic: Three illustrated faces flash for 2 seconds; a lineup of six appears and the thumb must tap the three shown. Faces share features to confuse recognition. Score is correct picks; dare-link sends the lineup to friends.
designs.video_trick: ego bait — 'I got all three, think you can?'

designs.name: MEMORY TRAIL
designs.conditioned_path: path-tracing / route-memory challenge clips where watching the trail is the hook
designs.mechanic: A dot traces a winding path across the canvas for 3 seconds; then trace the same path from memory with one finger. Deviation from the original path is scored in pixels — the PB is the tightest trace.
designs.video_trick: mastery flex — route memory tested with a real score

designs.name: NUMBER VAULT
designs.conditioned_path: digit-span recall games popularized as 'memory IQ' viral clips
designs.mechanic: A number string flashes for 1.5 seconds per two digits; then type it back in full. Length increases by one digit each round. Score is the longest string correctly recalled — the honest working-memory digit span.
designs.video_trick: benchmark ego bait — digit span as social currency

designs.name: SHADOW MATCH
designs.conditioned_path: 'guess the silhouette' nostalgia clips that bait the recognition snap with partial cues
designs.mechanic: A silhouette flashes for one second; six choices appear and the thumb taps the match. Silhouettes get more ambiguous each round and choices more similar. Streak ends on a miss — the brain\'s recognition memory exercised at real speed.
designs.video_trick: play-along comment bait — 'did you get it before the reveal?'

designs.name: DECODE RECALL
designs.conditioned_path: cryptic-clue / code-recall viral challenges that prove memory under pressure
designs.mechanic: A symbol-to-letter cipher is shown for 10 seconds; it hides and a word appears in symbols. Decode it from memory by tapping the letters. Longer words and more cipher entries ramp the working-memory load each round.
designs.video_trick: anticipation agony — 'can you hold the cipher long enough to crack the word?'

designs.name: SCENE REWIND
designs.conditioned_path: 'spot what changed between these two screenshots' nostalgia-diff posts
designs.mechanic: A scene of illustrated objects shows for 4 seconds; it resets with 3 objects removed and 2 added. Tap the objects that were NOT in the original scene. Score is correct identifications; dare-link prints the change count to beat.

designs.name: RHYTHM REPEAT
designs.conditioned_path: drum-pattern / handclap recall challenges where the rhythm plants in short-term memory
designs.mechanic: A tap-rhythm pattern plays as haptic pulses on screen; the thumb replays it by tapping. Pattern length grows each round. Miss-timing by more than 100ms ends the streak — sequence recall through the motor channel.
designs.video_trick: participation-comment-bait — 'can you feel the pattern and send it back?'

designs.name: MAP RECALL
designs.conditioned_path: viral 'place the flag / country quiz' recall posts that weaponize geography memory for shares
designs.mechanic: A simple illustrated map appears for 5 seconds with 5 marked spots; the map fades and the thumb must drag pins to the remembered positions. Distance from each original spot is scored — the dare unit is total error in map-units.
designs.video_trick: competitive rank — 'total error 47, think you can beat it?'

designs.name: MISSING TILE
designs.conditioned_path: 'what\'s wrong with this picture?' change-blindness clips that make the viewer feel foolish then share
designs.mechanic: A full grid of colored tiles shows for 3 seconds; one tile vanishes and the grid reshuffles. Tap the empty slot where the missing tile belongs. The grid grows each level and reshuffles more aggressively.
designs.video_trick: shock-honesty — 'change blindness is real, I missed it on round 4'

designs.name: STORY SEQUENCE
designs.conditioned_path: 'put this story in order' narrative-recall posts that spread on nostalgia for shared moments
designs.mechanic: Four illustrated story panels flash in order for 2 seconds each; they scramble and the thumb drags them back into the correct narrative sequence. Error count is zero or greater — a perfect run is the only dare worth sharing.
designs.video_trick: completion-satisfaction — the clean reorder lands like a solved puzzle

designs.name: RECALL GARDEN
designs.conditioned_path: 'on this day' memory-bait posts that pull the nostalgia reflex into a feed loop
designs.mechanic: A soft sequence of illustrated memory-motes (shapes, colors, small icons) floats past one at a time; after the last one, the thumb gently taps each that appeared, in any order. No timer, no miss penalty — the session ends when all are acknowledged and a garden tile blooms.
designs.video_trick: calm completion — the recall loop that resolves instead of repeating

designs.name: ECHO FADE
designs.conditioned_path: nostalgia-ASMR 'remember this sound?' posts that park a memory and reopen it gently
designs.mechanic: A short visual sequence pulses onto the screen, one element at a time, slowly; after it fades, the thumb traces the elements back in order at any pace. Each correct touch plays a soft chime. The sequence is short enough to always succeed — the exercise is pure gentle recall, not a test.
